title: Rich Malaysian Fettuccine Pasta
description: A sweet, juicy Malaysian-inspired fettuccine that balances tropical coconut milk with aromatic curry and fresh vegetables, perfect for a brunch mood.

ingredients.name: lemongrass stalk
ingredients.notes: pound with the back of a knife

instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil and cook the fettuccine according to package instructions until al dente; drain and set aside, reserving 1 cup of pasta water.
In a separate saucepan, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium heat, then add the ground lemongrass, red curry paste, and a splash of water, sautéing for 2 minutes until aromatic.
Pour in the unsweetened coconut milk, tamarind paste, soy sauce, and lime juice, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the sliced carrot and green beans, cover, and let the sauce slow‑cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Whisk the eggs in a bowl, then slowly pour the egg mixture into the simmering sauce while stirring constantly to create a velvety custard base.
Reduce the heat to low, letting the sauce thicken over 5 minutes; adjust thickness by adding reserved pasta water as needed.
Toss the cooked fettuccine into the sauce, ensuring each strand is coated and heated through.
Remove from heat, sprinkle fresh coriander and optional chili flakes, then drizzle a dash of lime zest for brightness.
Plate the fettuccine onto warmed bowls, arranging any remaining vegetables on top for visual appeal.
Serve immediately, offering extra lime wedges on the side for guests to squeeze.
